2605.17938.title: Training data attribution in diffusion models via mirrored unlearning and noise-consistent skew
2605.17938.paper_summary: Training data attribution (TDA) should enable generative model interpretability and foster a variety of related downstream tasks. Nonetheless, current TDA approaches lack reliability and robustness, preventing their adoption in real-world setups. In this paper, we take a decisive step towards more reliable and robust TDA for diffusion models. We propose to perform TDA with mirrored unlearning and noise-consistent skew (MUCS). The idea is to fine-tune a second model with bounded mirrored gradient ascent, and to measure the normalized skew of this model with respect to the original one using consistent noise samples. We show that, while being conceptually simple and generic, MUCS systematically outperforms existing methods on three different datasets by a large margin. We additionally study the effect that core design choices have on final performance, and analyze novel aspects regarding the overlap of influential instances across generated items and the potential of ensembling TDA approaches. We believe that our findings may have broader implications for more general unlearning setups, as well as for tasks requiring the comparison of diffusion losses.
2605.17938.paper_summary_zh: 訓練數據歸因（TDA）應該能夠促進生成模型的可解釋性，並促進各種相關的下游任務。<lb/>然而，當前的 TDA 方法缺乏可靠性和穩健性，阻礙了它們在現實世界中的應用。<lb/>在本文中，我們朝著更可靠和穩健的擴散模型 TDA 迈出了決定性的一步。<lb/>我們提出使用鏡像反學習和噪聲一致偏斜（MUCS）來執行 TDA。<lb/>這個想法是用有界的鏡像梯度上升來微調第二個模型，並使用一致的噪聲樣本來測量該模型相對於原始模型的標準化偏斜。<lb/>我們展示了 MUCS 在概念上雖然簡單且通用，但在三個不同數據集上系統性地超越現有方法，且差距顯著。<lb/>我們還研究了核心設計選擇對最終性能的影響，並分析了影響實例在生成項目之間重疊的創新方面以及集成 TDA 方法的潛力。<lb/>我們相信，我們的發現可能對更一般的反學習設置以及需要比較擴散損失的任務具有更廣泛的影響。
2605.17938.author: Joan Serrà et.al.
2605.17938.authors: Joan Serrà, Dipam Goswami, Fabio Morreale, Wei-Hsiang Liao, Yuki Mitsufuji
